submit.title: Senior Manager/Director - Data Lead
submit.jd_text:
Please note we are looking to speak to individuals already based in the Riyadh/Dubai as a first priority.

Capco, a Wipro company, is a global technology and management consultancy specializing in driving digital transformation in the financial services industry. With a growing client portfolio comprising of over 100 global organizations, Capco operates at the intersection of business and technology by combining innovative thinking with unrivalled industry knowledge to deliver end-to-end data-driven solutions and fast-track digital initiatives for banking and payments, capital markets, wealth and asset management, insurance, and the energy sector. Capco’s cutting-edge ingenuity is brought to life through its Innovation Labs and award-winning Be Yourself At Work culture and diverse talent.

Location: Riyadh/Dubai (On-site) | Practice Area: Data & AI | Type: Permanent

The Role

As a Managing Principal / Director – Data Lead, you’ll play a pivotal role in growing Capco’s Data & Analytics capability in Riyadh. This is a senior, hands-on leadership position that blends delivery ownership with market development. You’ll lead high-impact data programmes for major clients while actively contributing to sales activity, account growth, and the expansion of Capco’s footprint in the region.

What You’ll Do

Lead end-to-end delivery of complex data and analytics engagements, remaining hands-on enough to guide technical direction and outcomesAct as a trusted senior advisor to client stakeholders, translating business objectives into scalable data solutionsDrive business development activity, supporting proposals, shaping client opportunities, and building long-term client relationshipsBuild and grow Capco’s Data & Analytics presence in Riyadh, contributing to capability development and local market strategyMentor and develop teams, fostering an inclusive, high-performing environment aligned to Capco values

What We’re Looking For

Experience in leading Consulting firms10+ years’ practical experience in data, analytics or AI roles with significant, demonstrable exposure to the financial services industrySenior leadership experience delivering large-scale data and analytics programmes within consulting or complex client environmentsStrong technical grounding across data platforms, architecture, and analytics, with the ability to engage credibly in delivery discussionsExcellent stakeholder management skills, with confidence working at executive levelDemonstrable consulting background and experience in delivery or sales, account growth and client development.

Bonus Points For

Experience working in or across Middle Eastern marketsBackground in financial services or adjacent regulated industriesExperience shaping new market offerings or growing regional practicesNative Arabic speaker

Why Join Capco

Deliver high-impact data and analytics solutions for leading organisationsPlay a key role in growing Capco’s Data & AI in the Middle EastWork in a collaborative, flat, and entrepreneurial consulting culture

Inclusion at Capco

We’re committed to making our recruitment process accessible and straightforward for everyone. If you need any adjustments at any stage, just let us know – we’ll be happy to help. We value each person’s unique perspective and contribution. At Capco, we believe that being yourself is your greatest strength. Our #BeYourselfAtWork culture encourages individuality and collaboration – a mindset that shapes how we work with clients and each other every day.

submit.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) - Saudi Nationals
submit.jd_text: Role Overview The Chief Operating Officer will serve as the second-in-command, overseeing all day-to-day operational aspects of the company. As a newly licensed EMI under SAMA, the COO will ensure scalable, compliant, and efficient operations for payment acquiring, digital wallet services, and money transfers targeted at SMEs. The role focuses on translating strategy into execution, driving operational excellence, ensuring regulatory adherence, managing risk, and supporting rapid growth in the Saudi fintech ecosystem. Key Responsibilities ▪ Develop and implement operational strategies aligned with the company’s business objectives, growth targets, and SAMA regulatory framework for EMIs. ▪ Oversee core operations including payment processing, transaction acquiring, digital wallet onboarding, fund transfers, merchant services, customer support, and back-office functions. ▪ Ensure full compliance with SAMA regulations, including anti-money laundering (AML), counter-terrorism financing (CTF), data protection, cybersecurity, customer due diligence (CDD), and ongoing reporting requirements. ▪ Lead cross-functional teams (operations, compliance, risk, technology, customer success) to optimize processes, reduce costs, and enhance service reliability for SME clients. ▪ Build and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs), operational dashboards, SLAs, and reporting mechanisms for transaction success rates, uptime, fraud prevention, and customer satisfaction. ▪ Manage vendor and partner relationships (e.g., payment schemes, banks, processors, technology providers) to ensure seamless integration and performance. ▪ Drive continuous process improvement, automation, and technology adoption (e.g., AI for fraud detection, scalable cloud infrastructure) to support business scaling. ▪ Oversee risk management, operational resilience, business continuity planning, and incident response in line with SAMA guidelines. ▪ Collaborate with the CEO and leadership team on budgeting, resource allocation, forecasting, and strategic planning. ▪ Foster a high-performance culture focused on compliance, innovation, and customer- centric operations. Requirements ▪ Bachelor’s degree (Master’s/MBA preferred) in Business Administration, Finance, Operations Management, or related field. ▪ 10+ years of progressive leadership experience in fintech, payments, digital banking; prior EMI, PI, PSP, or payment acquiring experience strongly preferred. ▪ Proven track record as a COO or senior operations leader in a regulated financial environment. ▪ Deep understanding of payment systems, digital wallets, merchant acquiring, transaction processing, and fintech regulations in Saudi Arabia. ▪ Strong knowledge of SAMA’s EMI/Payment Services regulations, AML/CTF, and cybersecurity standards. ▪ Excellent leadership, strategic thinking, and change management skills. ▪ Experience scaling operations in a high-growth fintech startup/scale-up environment. ▪ Fluency in English and Arabic (spoken and written). ▪ Saudi nationals with strong local market knowledge.

submit.title: Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation
submit.jd_text:
Remote | Global Role | Multi-Industry

This is not a traditional asset management role

This role is designed for someone who understands that the future of maintenance and asset management is driven by data, reliability engineering, and intelligent systems.

If your experience is rooted in Reliability-Centred Maintenance (RCM), preventive and predictive strategies, labour optimisation, and cost control, and you are interested in applying this knowledge to AI-driven solutions, this is a unique opportunity to operate at the forefront of the industry.

About MaxAccelerate Technology Group

MaxAccelerate Technology Group is a global technology and transformation business focused on helping asset-intensive organisations improve performance, reduce cost, and scale operations through data, systems, and AI.

Through our ecosystem, including DataMax AI, we work with organisations across Facilities Management, Transportation, Pharma, Manufacturing, Energy, and Infrastructure to transform how assets are managed.

Our approach is grounded in real operational understanding:

 Maintenance strategies must be structured, measurable, and optimised  Systems such as IBM Maximo and other EAM platforms must deliver actionable insight  AI must be applied in a way that drives tangible outcomes across cost, labour, and asset performance 

The Role

We are seeking a Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation to lead the definition and delivery of AI-driven asset management solutions.

The focus of the role is to take deep operational knowledge — including:

 RCM frameworks  Preventive and condition-based maintenance strategies  Workforce planning and labour models  Spare parts, inventory, and asset lifecycle considerations 

and translate these into structured AI use cases and scalable solutions.

Key Responsibilities

 Work closely with Heads of Maintenance, Engineering Leaders, and Operations teams to understand:  Maintenance strategies and maturity  Preventive vs reactive ratios  Labour utilisation and workforce efficiency  Spare parts management and inventory control  Define and structure AI use cases that address:  Reduction in reactive maintenance  Optimisation of preventive maintenance schedules  Labour efficiency and workforce planning  Spare parts forecasting and cost reduction  Asset lifecycle optimisation  Develop and lead Proof of Concepts (PoCs) that validate:  Operational improvements  Cost savings  Performance uplift  Translate business requirements into clear scope of works for DataMax AI R&D teams  Ensure AI solutions integrate effectively with EAM/CMMS platforms (Maximo preferred) and align with real operational workflows  Build clear ROI models, ensuring every solution delivers measurable impact across:  Maintenance costs  Asset uptime  Labour efficiency  Inventory and working capital  Support clients in adopting solutions that improve their infrastructure, operations, and long-term asset strategy 

Why This Role Matters

Most organisations have:

 Maintenance strategies defined on paper  Systems implemented but underutilised  Data available but not fully leveraged 

This role exists to bridge that gap.

You will ensure that:

 Maintenance frameworks such as RCM are not just theoretical, but digitised and optimised  Preventive strategies are continuously refined based on real performance data  Labour and cost structures are aligned with actual operational demand  AI is applied in a way that improves real infrastructure outcomes, not just reporting 

What We Are Looking For
